description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Wakayama Prefecture (和歌山県, Wakayama-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [wa.kaꜜ.ja.ma, wa.ka.ja.maꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Kansai region of Honshu.

description: newspaper in San Francisco, California
description_wiki: The Nichi Bei Times (日米タイムズ Nichi Bei Taimuzu) is a Japanese American news agency operated by the Nichi Bei Foundation and headquartered in San Francisco.

surface_forms: San Jose Mercury in the morning
description: daily newspaper published in San Jose, California, United States, since 1851
description_wiki: The Mercury News (formerly San Jose Mercury News; often locally known as The Merc) is a morning daily newspaper published in San Jose, California, in the San Francisco Bay Area.

description: daily newspaper published in San Jose, California, United States, since 1851
city in San Mateo County, California, United States
description_wiki: Menlo Park ( MEN-loh) is a city in San Mateo County in the San Francisco Bay Area of California, United States.

description: city in San Mateo County, California, United States
description_wiki: Redwood City is a city in San Mateo County, California, on the San Francisco Peninsula in the Bay Area of Northern California, approximately 27 miles (43 km) south of San Francisco and 24 miles (39 km) northwest of San Jose.

description: city in San Mateo County, California, United States
description_wiki: East Palo Alto ( EAST PAL-oh AL-toh; abbreviated E.P.A.) is a city in San Mateo County, California, United States.

description: city in Santa Clara County, California, United States
description_wiki: Palo Alto ( PAL-oh AL-toh; Spanish for 'tall stick') is a charter city in northwestern Santa Clara County, California, United States, in the San Francisco Bay Area, named after a coastal redwood tree known as El Palo Alto.

description: city in and county seat of Santa Barbara County, California, United States
description_wiki: Santa Barbara (Spanish: Santa Bárbara, meaning 'Saint Barbara') is a coastal city in Santa Barbara County, California, of which it is also the county seat.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description: city in Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Fremont () is a city in Alameda County, California, United States.

description: a makeshift concentration camp set up by the U.S. Army in 1942 to incarcerate people of Japanese ancestry
description_wiki: The Tanforan Assembly Center was created to temporarily detain nearly 8,000 Japanese Americans, mostly from the San Francisco Bay Area, under the auspices of Executive Order 9066.

description: Internment camp for Japanese Americans in Millard County, Utah, United States
description_wiki: The Topaz War Relocation Center, also known as the Central Utah Relocation Center (Topaz) and briefly as the Abraham Relocation Center, was an American concentration camp in which Americans of Japanese descent and immigrants who had come to the United States from Japan, called Nikkei were incarcerated.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Montana (  mon-TAN-ə) is a landlocked state in the Mountain West subregion of the Western United States.

description: city in Contra Costa County, California, United States
description_wiki: Walnut Creek is a city in Contra Costa County, California, United States, located in the East Bay region of the San Francisco Bay Area, about 16 miles (26 kilometers) east of the city of Oakland, California and 25 miles (40 kilometers) east of San Francisco.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Okumura (written: 奥村) is a Japanese surname.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: city in Santa Clara County, California, United States
description_wiki: Mountain View is a city in Santa Clara County, California, United States, part of the San Francisco Bay Area.

description: technology park in Palo Alto, California, USA
description_wiki: Stanford Research Park (SRP) is a technology park established in 1951 as a joint initiative between Stanford University and the City of Palo Alto.

aliases: Pueblo de San José de Guadalupe
Pueblo de San Jose de Guadalupe
description: city and county seat of Santa Clara County, California, United States
description_wiki: San Jose, officially the City of San José (  SAN hoh-ZAY, -⁠SAY; Spanish: [saŋ xoˈse]), is the most populous city in the San Francisco Bay Area and Northern California, and the 12th-most populous in the United States, with 997,368 residents.

entity: Japanese American Museum of San Jose
description: museum in San Jose, California
description_wiki: The Japanese American Museum of San Jose (JAMsj) is located at 535 N. Fifth Street in San Jose, in the heart of Japantown.
